name: PHP - Laravel
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in PHP - Laravel should be able to independently design and deliver medium-sized backend services and features in a production Laravel application: design relational schemas with proper indexes and foreign-key constraints, implement and fix Eloquent relationships including polymorphic and many-to-many with pivot columns, resolve N+1 query problems using eager loading and query optimisation, bind interfaces to concrete implementations in the service container, write and register middleware, events, listeners, queued jobs, notifications, caching with the Cache facade (remember, tags, TTL, invalidation), gate/policy-based authorisation, Eloquent API resources and resource collections, and the repository pattern. They write meaningful PHPUnit and Pest tests using factories, RefreshDatabase, mocking via Mockery/PHPUnit mocks, and fake helpers (Mail, Storage, Queue, Notification, Http). They refactor legacy controller-heavy code into layered service/repository architectures, tune queries using EXPLAIN and Eloquent debug tools, configure service providers, write custom Artisan commands with argument/option parsing and progress bars, and make sound decisions on transactional boundaries, exception hierarchy design, cache key strategies, and queue retry policies.
long_scope:
INTERMEDIATE PHP - Laravel – Competency Scope (3 – 5 years professional experience)

An engineer operating at this level is expected to work with limited supervision on medium-sized modules, contribute to overall system quality, and reliably extend or refactor production Laravel code written by others. Depth in core Laravel conventions is already secure; breadth now expands to the full service-container ecosystem, advanced Eloquent patterns, background processing, caching, authorisation, and testability.

────────────────────────────────────────
1. Eloquent Relationships & Data Access
• Implements all standard Eloquent relationships: hasOne, hasMany, belongsTo, belongsToMany (with pivot columns and withPivot), hasManyThrough, morphTo/morphMany/morphToMany.
• Resolves N+1 query problems using eager loading (with(), load()) and lazy eager loading; uses withCount() for efficient aggregation.
• Authors and uses query scopes (local and global), Eloquent observers, and model events (creating, updated, deleting).
• Tunes queries with EXPLAIN, Eloquent query log (DB::enableQueryLog()), and covering indexes; uses cursor() and chunk() for large dataset iteration.
• Applies raw expressions, subqueries, and cross-model joins via the query builder; uses DB::transaction() for atomic writes.

2. Service Container, Providers & Dependency Injection
• Binds interfaces to concrete implementations in a service provider using $this->app->bind(), singleton(), and instance().
• Authors and registers custom service providers (boot() vs. register()); defers provider loading when appropriate.
• Uses contextual binding to inject different implementations for different consumers.
• Understands the container resolution lifecycle and shared vs. non-shared bindings.

3. Middleware, Events & Background Processing
• Writes before/after and terminable middleware; applies middleware selectively to route groups and individual routes.
• Designs event/listener pairs, registers them in EventServiceProvider, and uses event() / Event::dispatch() from the application layer.
• Implements queued jobs with handle(), failed(), and retry policies (tries, backoff, maxExceptions); dispatches to named queues.
• Uses Laravel Horizon or Telescope for queue monitoring at a configuration and interpretation level.
• Sends notifications via the mail and database channels; writes custom notification classes.

4. Caching
• Uses Cache::remember(), Cache::rememberForever(), Cache::forget(), Cache::has() with deterministic keys derived from request context.
• Applies cache tags for grouped invalidation where the driver supports them; designs TTL strategy appropriate to data freshness requirements.
• Understands cache stampede risks and basic mitigation (atomic locks via Cache::lock()).
• Selects appropriate cache drivers (Redis, Memcached, database, array for tests) based on scenario requirements.

5. Authorisation
• Defines Gates and Policies; registers policies via AuthServiceProvider or model discovery.
• Uses $this->authorize(), Gate::allows/denies, and @can in Blade without exposing internals in HTTP responses.
• Designs a coherent authorisation strategy distinguishing authentication checks (auth middleware) from permission checks (policies/gates).

6. API Resources & Response Design
• Builds Eloquent API Resources and Resource Collections to decouple model shape from API contract.
• Conditionally includes attributes and relationships with whenLoaded() and when().
• Designs versioned API responses and consistent error envelopes.

7. Repository Pattern & Service Layer
• Extracts data-access logic into repository classes behind interfaces, binds them in a service provider, and injects via constructor.
• Separates domain/application/infrastructure concerns: controllers orchestrate, services hold business rules, repositories own queries.
• Refactors bloated controllers and fat models into this layered structure without breaking existing tests.

8. Custom Artisan Commands
• Writes Artisan commands with argument and option definitions ($signature), interactive I/O (ask, confirm, info, error), and progress bars (withProgressBar()).
• Adds --dry-run, --force, and similar operational options; handles signals and long-running iteration safely.
• Registers commands in the Kernel; schedules them via the task scheduler with appropriate frequency and overlap prevention (withoutOverlapping()).

9. Testing at Intermediate Depth
• Writes focused PHPUnit and Pest feature tests: actingAs, assertStatus, assertJson, assertJsonFragment, assertDatabaseHas, assertDatabaseMissing.
• Mocks service dependencies using Mockery or PHPUnit mocks; injects mocks via the container override (app()->instance()).
• Uses Http::fake() to stub external HTTP calls; asserts dispatched jobs, sent mail, queued notifications, and fired events.
• Applies model factories with states and sequences; seeds deterministic test data without relying on production datasets.

10. Exception Design & HTTP Mapping
• Designs a custom exception hierarchy (domain exceptions extending RuntimeException); maps exceptions to stable HTTP status codes and JSON error shapes in the exception Handler.
• Uses report() and render() separation; avoids leaking stack traces or internal messages to API clients.
• Handles PDO/Eloquent exceptions (ModelNotFoundException → 404, AuthorizationException → 403, ValidationException → 422) centrally.

11. Performance & Query Optimisation
• Reads and interprets EXPLAIN output; adds composite indexes to support common query patterns.
• Eliminates N+1 via eager loading; avoids SELECT * in tight loops; uses paginate()/cursor() instead of get() on unbounded result sets.
• Profiles with Laravel Debugbar, Telescope, or query log; identifies hot paths and proposes targeted improvements.

12. Maintenance & Collaboration
• Adheres to PSR-12, Laravel coding style, and PHPDoc conventions; enforces style via PHP-CS-Fixer or PHPCS in CI.
• Participates in code reviews; raises migration safety concerns (zero-downtime, reversible changes); documents schema decisions in ADRs.
• Manages semantic versioning for internal packages; handles PHP and Laravel version upgrades responsibly.

────────────────────────────────────────

An interviewer evaluating an INTERMEDIATE PHP - Laravel developer should probe across all of the above domains, expecting confident explanations grounded in production experience, practical anecdotes, and code-level reasoning at the module scale.
